section.heading: Use of Department of Defense funds for United States share of costs of United Nations peacekeeping activities: limitation

subsection.heading: Prohibition on Use of Funds.—
subsection.chapeau: Funds available to the Department of Defense may not be used to make a financial contribution (directly or through another department or agency of the United States) to the United Nations—

paragraph.content: for the costs of a United Nations peacekeeping activity; or
for any United States arrearage to the United Nations.

subsection.heading: Application of Prohibition.—
subsection.content: The prohibition in subsection (a) applies to voluntary contributions, as well as to contributions pursuant to assessment by the United Nations for the United States share of the costs of a peacekeeping activity.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 104–106, div. A, title XIII, § 1301(a), Feb. 10, 1996, 110 Stat. 473.)

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 405 was renumbered section 401(e) of this title.

note.heading: Use of Department of Defense Funds for United Nations Forces
note.p:
Pub. L. 105–261, div. A, title XII, § 1231(b), Oct. 17, 1998, 112 Stat. 2155, provided that: “No funds available to the Department of Defense may be used— “(1) for a monetary contribution to the United Nations for the establishment of a standing international force under the United Nations; or
“(2) to assign or detail any member of the Armed Forces to duty with a United Nations Stand By Force.”
